messages.subject: [RsR] Pictures from Banff
messages.body_plain:
After having updated 'rrcov' I feel like updating my picture album from Banff.

Enjoy:

http://picasaweb.google.at/valentin.to/RSRBanff2007

All pictures are available from the authors in full resolution, as
well as printed or as a poster in any size, upon request.

Best regards,
Valentin

messages.subject: [RsR] useR! 2008 submission deadline - robustness talks!
messages.body_plain:
Dear R-Robustniks,

I just wanted to remind you of the submission deadline
(and early registration deadline!) of 
2008-03-31
==========  --> Website http://www.R-project.org/useR-2008/

useR! 2008, the R user conference, takes place at ... TU Dortmund, Germany
from 2008-08-12 to 2008-08-14. 
Pre-conference tutorials will take place on August 11.

As some of you have heard already, one of the half-day tutorials
   http://www.statistik.uni-dortmund.de/useR-2008/tutorials/
will be by Professor Elvezio Ronchetti and me with a
     "Introduction to Robust Statistics with R"
with the goals to
- "propagandize" robust statistics to the active R community
- making use of the many robustness-related R packages.
  {of which there should be a CRAN-task view "Real Soon Now"}

Please consider submitting a paper in time, in order to help
making "robustness" an important subject at the meeting.
Note that Ursula Gather, Roland Fried and other 'robustniks' are
part of the local organizing statistics department; another good
reason to be there this summer!

Martin Maechler, ETH Zurich
for the program committee, useR! 2008

messages.subject: [RsR] Least Trimmed Square(LTS) estimation in R
messages.subject_clean: [RsR] Least Trimmed Square(LTS) estimation in R
messages.body_plain:
Hi Every One, I am novice to R and am writing programe for Least trimmed 
square(LTS) estimation in R. if anyone of you have done it already or if 
there is somthing that can give me some more background about LTS 
Regression. please let me know.

Thanks,
Dost M Khan
khanx131 at umn.edu
messages.body_snippet:
Hi Every One, I am novice to R and am writing programe for Least trimmed 
square(LTS) estimation in R. if anyone of you have done it already or if 
there is somthing that can give me some more backgro

messages.subject: [RsR] Least Trimmed Square(LTS) estimation in R
messages.subject_clean: [RsR] Least Trimmed Square(LTS) estimation in R
messages.body_plain:
Dear Dost,

install package 'robustbase' and have a look at ltsReg(), i.e.

install.packages("robustbase")
library(robustbase)
?ltsReg

The help page will give you some references to start with.

Hope this helps,

Best regards,
Valentin



----- Original Message ----- 
From: <khanx131 at umn.edu>
To: <r-sig-robust at r-project.org>
Cc: <khanx131 at umn.edu>
Sent: Friday, March 21, 2008 9:51 AM
Subject: [RsR] Least Trimmed Square(LTS) estimation in R


Hi Every One, I am novice to R and am writing programe for Least trimmed 
square(LTS) estimation in R. if anyone of you have done it already or if 
there is somthing that can give me some more background about LTS 
Regression. please let me know.

Thanks,
Dost M Khan
khanx131 at umn.edu

_______________________________________________
R-SIG-Robust at r-project.org mailing list
https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/r-sig-robust

messages.subject: [RsR] Least Trimmed Square(LTS) estimation in R
messages.subject_clean: [RsR] Least Trimmed Square(LTS) estimation in R
messages.body_plain:
The package robustbase contains an LTS impementation. More information  
about the algorithm may be found in the help of the package. The  
package RFreak also contains an LTS algorithm, which is mainly  
intended for complicated data situations.

Best Regards,
Robin Nunkesser

messages.subject: [RsR] Least Trimmed Square(LTS) estimation in R
messages.subject_clean: [RsR] Least Trimmed Square(LTS) estimation in R
messages.body_plain:
Hello Dost,

No need to do it yourself. Good implementations of LTS estimators have been
publicly available for a long time. 

Currently, they are available in R as part of the robustbase library, among others.

> install.packages('robustbase')
> library(robustbase)
> help(ltsReg)

should get you going.

Matias
